title: Safety taxonomy + controlled vocabularies (hazards/PPE/waste/biosafety)
context: Machine-readable, consistent safety data requires controlled vocabularies so that hazard classes, PPE requirements, waste categories, and biosafety levels are never free text. Without standardized terms aligned to GHS, NIH/CDC BMBL, OSHA/EPA, and institutional waste categories, the linter cannot enforce safety completeness and the schema cannot validate hazard coverage reliably. This vocabulary is a prerequisite for authoring any safety-bearing protocol. Depends on schema-001 (schema-001 defines the fields; vocab-004 defines the valid values).
objective: Define and publish the controlled vocabularies for hazard classes, PPE, waste disposal, and biosafety levels as YAML files aligned to GHS, NIH/CDC BMBL, OSHA/EPA, and standard waste categories, suitable for reference by the JSON Schema and the linter.
acceptanceCriteria: vocab/hazards.yaml covers GHS hazard classes and pictograms with authoritative source citations.
vocab/ppe.yaml covers standard PPE types (gloves, gowns, goggles, respirators, face shields) aligned to OSHA/NIOSH categories.
vocab/waste.yaml covers chemical, biological, sharps, and radioactive waste categories aligned to EPA/CDC/OSHA guidance.
vocab/biosafety-levels.yaml covers BSL-1 through BSL-4 per NIH/CDC BMBL definitions.
Each vocabulary entry cites its authoritative source (standard, edition, section); commit DCO signed-off.
Vocabularies reviewed and signed off by credentialed safety reviewer (or annotated for review pending reviewer-002 completion).
resources: GHS Purple Book (UN Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals) — https://unece.org/ghs
ECHA Chemical classification and labelling — https://echa.europa.eu/
output: Committed YAML controlled vocabularies (vocab/hazards.yaml, vocab/ppe.yaml, vocab/waste.yaml, vocab/biosafety-levels.yaml) with authoritative source citations per entry, aligned to GHS/BMBL/OSHA/EPA; commit DCO signed-off.
